description: Fires when a visitor scrolls down to a specific point on the page (e.g. 50% of the page or a given number of pixels). By default the tag fires on all pages; add conditions to fire only on a certain page or under specific criteria. You must specify the scroll threshold using the built-in Page Scroll variable and set the condition options.unit to 'percentage' or 'pixels'. See Piwik PRO help: Page scroll trigger.

scroll_condition.description: Fires the tag when a visitor scrolls to the selected point on the page. For example, a visitor scrolls 50% of the page or scrolls down to 1440 px.

builtin_variable.name: Page Scroll
builtin_variable.description: A variable used in the page scroll trigger. It defines the scroll depth threshold at which the tag fires.
builtin_variable.usage: Use this variable_id in the condition that defines when the trigger fires. The condition_type for this condition must be set to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session. Set the condition value to the threshold (percentage 0–100 when unit is percentage, or pixel value when unit is pixels). The condition options object must contain a field 'unit' with value 'percentage' or 'pixels'.
builtin_variable.condition_type_description: For the Page Scroll condition, condition_type must be one of these (not equals or other comparison).
builtin_variable.value_range: When unit is 'percentage': 0–100. When unit is 'pixels': scroll depth in pixels (e.g. 1440).

unit.description: Unit for the scroll threshold. 'percentage' = percent of page scrolled (value 0–100). 'pixels' = scroll depth in pixels.

multiplicity: Many times during a session (each time)
page_scroll_conditions_reference.note: The scroll condition must use the built-in variable 'Page Scroll' (id a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5). Set condition_type to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session. Set value to the threshold (0–100 for percentage, or pixel number for pixels). Condition options must include unit: 'percentage' or 'pixels'.

condition_types.description: Fires when a visitor scrolls on a page with the selected page URL to the selected point
Fires when a visitor scrolls on a page with the selected page path to the selected point
Fires when a visitor scrolls on a page hosted on the selected domain to the selected point
Fires when a visitor scrolls on a page to which they were directed by the selected internal referrer and scrolls to the selected point
Fires when a visitor scrolls on a page to which they were directed by the selected external referrer and scrolls to the selected point
Fires when a visitor scrolls on a page to which they were directed by the selected campaign (utm_campaign) and scrolls to the selected point
Fires when a visitor scrolls on a page to which they were directed by the selected traffic source. Values: campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct
Fires when a visitor is a returning visitor and scrolls to the selected point
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

when_to_use: Track scroll depth (e.g. 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%) for content engagement
Fire tags when the user reaches a specific section of the page
Measure how far visitors scroll before leaving or converting
common_use_cases: Lazy-load or trigger content when user scrolls to a point
Conversion or survey when user reaches bottom of article
best_practices: Use options.unit 'percentage' and value 0–100 for percent-based thresholds
Use options.unit 'pixels' and value in pixels for fixed scroll depth
Add Page path conditions to limit the trigger to specific pages; set multiplicity (e.g. once per page view) to avoid duplicate events

create_trigger.description: Create a page scroll trigger. Requires at least one condition using the built-in 'Page Scroll' variable (variable_id a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5) with condition_type set to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session; value set to the threshold (0–100 for percentage, or pixels); options.unit set to 'percentage' or 'pixels'; optionally add event conditions.

trigger_type.description: Must be 'page_scroll' for page scroll triggers

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Include at least one condition with variable_id a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5 (Page Scroll), condition_type = each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session, value = threshold (0–100 for percentage or pixel number), and options = { "unit": "percentage" } or { "unit": "pixels" }. Optional event conditions.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing page scroll trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include at least one condition using the built-in variable 'Page Scroll' (variable_id a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5) with condition_type = each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session; value = threshold (0–100 when unit is percentage, or pixel value when unit is pixels); options.unit = 'percentage' or 'pixels'. May include event conditions.

structure.variable_id: For scroll threshold use a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5 (Page Scroll); for event conditions use the appropriate variable UUID
structure.condition_type: For the Page Scroll condition (variable_id a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5) must be one of: each_time, once_per_page_view, once_per_session. For event conditions use the appropriate type (e.g. equals, contains).
structure.value: When unit is percentage: number 0–100 (e.g. 50). When unit is pixels: number of pixels (e.g. 1440)

conditions.recommendation: Include one condition with variable_id a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5 (Page Scroll), condition_type each_time | once_per_page_view | once_per_session, value 0–100 for percentage (or pixel number), and options: { "unit": "percentage" } or { "unit": "pixels" }; add Page path or Page URL conditions to limit where the trigger fires.

page_scroll_50_percent.description: Fire when user scrolls to 50% of the page (percentage unit)

page_scroll_75_percent.description: Fire when user scrolls to 75% (percentage unit)

page_scroll_pixels.description: Fire when user scrolls down to 1440 pixels

common_mistakes: Using condition_type other than each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session for the Page Scroll condition
Omitting options.unit or using a value other than 'percentage' or 'pixels'
Using a value outside 0–100 when unit is 'percentage'
Omitting the Page Scroll condition so the trigger has no scroll threshold

possible_causes: condition_type not set to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session for the Page Scroll condition
Value format wrong for chosen unit
debugging_steps: Ensure one condition uses variable_id a9a87b7d-d565-433d-8090-df9d80bf91f5 with condition_type each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session and options: { "unit": "percentage" } or { "unit": "pixels" }
Use value 0–100 for percentage; integer for pixels
Test with only the Page Scroll condition
